notes:
Tower balance philosophy:
- Offensive towers are the primary damage dealers. Each should have a clear role and feel distinct.
- Support towers multiply effectiveness of offensive towers but are useless alone.
- Resource towers enable late-game scaling.
- Range reference: short=6, medium=12, long=20 grid units.
- All towers can hit ground and air.
- Towers have passive regen (1% max HP / 5 sec) plus Repair Drone healing.
- 75% sell-back value.

Upgrade branching creates meaningful choices -- you commit a tower to a specialization path.

offensive.name: Autocannon Turret
offensive.description: Bread-and-butter defensive tower. High fire rate shreds swarm enemies. Low damage per shot but sustained DPS is strong against low-armor targets. Falls off against armored enemies late game unless upgraded.
offensive.visualDescription: Compact rotating turret mounted on a square armored base with bolted panel construction. Dual parallel barrels with visible rifling spin up before firing. Matte gunmetal grey finish with orange-yellow muzzle flash and ejecting brass casings. Tracer rounds visible as bright orange streaks. Impact effect: small spark burst on target. Idle animation: turret slowly scans left-right searching for targets. Firing animation: barrel rotation accelerates, sustained muzzle flash, light barrel glow from heat during sustained fire. Gatling branch: barrels replaced with a 6-barrel minigun assembly, higher spin speed, more intense muzzle flash. Heavy Autocannon branch: single larger barrel, visible recoil kickback, larger shell casings, deeper muzzle flash with smoke ring.

upgrades.name: Reinforced Barrel
upgrades.statChanges: +4 damage (16 total)

upgrades.name: Rapid Cycling
upgrades.statChanges: +1 attack speed (5 total), +50 HP

upgrades.name: Advanced Armaments
upgrades.statChanges: +4 damage (20 total), +1 armor. Branch point.

upgrades.name: Specialization
upgrades.statChanges: Gatling: +3 atk speed, +2 dmg. Heavy Auto: +15 dmg, -1 atk speed, +1 splash radius.

offensive.name: Missile Battery
offensive.description: Fires guided missiles that explode on impact. The go-to tower for dealing with mixed groups of enemies. Splash damage makes it efficient against clumped enemies but overkill against singles. Pairs well with barriers that funnel enemies together.
offensive.visualDescription: Boxy armored launcher pod with 4 angled missile tubes arranged in a 2x2 grid. Dark olive green painted metal with red-tipped warheads visible inside the open tubes. Missiles launch with a visible smoke trail and arc through the air in a ballistic curve before impacting with a fiery orange-red explosion and expanding shockwave ring. After firing, tubes show brief smoke wisps and reload with a mechanical click animation (warheads rise into position). Idle animation: launcher pod slowly rotates to track nearest threat, status light blinks green. Cluster Rocket branch: tubes replaced with 8 smaller launch ports, fires salvos of mini-rockets that scatter on impact. Siege Missile branch: single oversized tube with a larger warhead, missile is visibly bigger with a longer exhaust trail and more devastating ground-shaking impact.

upgrades.name: Expanded Payload
upgrades.statChanges: +10 damage (55 total), +0.5 splash radius

upgrades.name: Tracking Systems
upgrades.statChanges: +0.2 attack speed (1.0 total), +2 range (14 total)

upgrades.name: Warhead Research
upgrades.statChanges: +15 damage (70 total), +100 HP. Branch point.

upgrades.name: Specialization
upgrades.statChanges: Cluster Rockets: +1.5 splash radius, +80% splash dmg. Siege Missile: +50 dmg, -1 splash radius, +4 range.

offensive.name: Rail Gun
offensive.description: Fires a hypersonic penetrating round at extreme range. Highest single-target damage of any basic tower. Excels at sniping high-value targets like Bile Spitters, Gorgers, and softening bosses. Slow fire rate means it wastes damage on small enemies.
offensive.visualDescription: Long sleek barrel mounted on a low-profile swiveling base with stabilizer struts. Matte black metallic finish with luminous blue energy conduits running along the barrel length. Before each shot, energy visibly builds from base to muzzle tip over 0.5 seconds (blue glow intensifying along conduits), then fires with a bright blue-white flash and visible recoil that briefly tilts the barrel upward. Projectile is an instantaneous blue-white beam trail (hitscan visual) that lingers for 0.2 seconds. Impact effect: bright penetration spark with metallic debris. Pierce upgrade adds visible exit-wound spark on the far side of the first target. Idle animation: barrel makes micro-adjustments tracking potential targets, conduits pulse with a slow blue energy heartbeat. No branch visual variants needed (linear upgrade path).

upgrades.name: Accelerator Coils
upgrades.statChanges: +30 damage (120 total), +2 range (20 total)

upgrades.name: Piercing Rounds
upgrades.statChanges: Shots pierce through first target hitting a second for 50% damage. +20 damage (140 total).

upgrades.name: Supercharged
upgrades.statChanges: +60 damage (200 total), +0.2 attack speed (0.7 total), +100 HP

offensive.name: Plasma Mortar
offensive.role: Long range heavy AoE, area denial
offensive.description: Lobs superheated plasma in a high arc, creating a devastating explosion on impact. Very slow fire rate but each shot punishes clumped enemies. The plasma pool persists for 2 seconds dealing burn damage. Best tower for area denial and punishing large pushes.
offensive.visualDescription: wide mortar tube on a reinforced 2x2 platform, visible plasma charge glowing orange-white before arcing high into the air, heavy industrial look with heat venting pipes, impact creates a visible burning pool

upgrades.name: Superheated Plasma
upgrades.statChanges: +20 damage (90 total), burn pool damage 10/sec for 2 seconds

upgrades.name: Extended Chamber
upgrades.statChanges: +1 splash radius (4 total), +4 range (20 total), +0.1 attack speed

upgrades.name: Inferno Core
upgrades.statChanges: +40 damage (130 total), burn pool lasts 4 seconds, +200 HP

offensive.name: Tesla Coil
offensive.description: Releases electric arcs that chain between nearby enemies. Each bolt jumps to up to 3 additional targets within 4 grids of each other. Exceptional against swarms and mixed groups where enemies are close together. Damage is consistent regardless of armor, making it effective against armored targets too.
offensive.visualDescription: tall conical coil crackling with visible electricity, blue-white arcs leap between the coil tip and targets, base has capacitor banks that visibly charge between shots, metallic silver with blue energy glow

upgrades.name: Arc Amplifier
upgrades.statChanges: +8 damage (33 total), chains to +1 target (4 total)

upgrades.name: Capacitor Bank
upgrades.statChanges: +0.5 attack speed (2.0 total), +2 range (12 total)

upgrades.name: Overcharge
upgrades.statChanges: +12 damage (45 total), +100 HP. Branch point.

upgrades.name: Specialization
upgrades.statChanges: Storm Spire: chains to 6 targets, +4 range. Arc Cannon: no chain, +80 damage single target, +0.5 atk speed.

abilities.name: Chain Lightning
abilities.description: Each attack chains to up to 3 additional enemies within 4 grid units of the primary target. Chain targets take 75% of the original damage. Cannot chain back to already-hit targets.

offensive.name: Inferno Tower
offensive.role: Short range continuous beam, sustained DPS
offensive.description: Projects a continuous heat beam that locks onto a single target and melts it down. Highest sustained DPS of any tower at close range. Damage ramps up the longer it stays on the same target, rewarding placement at chokepoints where enemies are forced to walk through its beam. Vulnerable due to short range.
offensive.visualDescription: squat heavily armored cylinder with a glowing red-orange emitter on top, continuous visible beam connects to target, emitter glows brighter as damage ramps up, dark iron body with heat distortion effect around it

upgrades.name: Focused Lens
upgrades.statChanges: +3 damage (11 total), ramp bonus +50% faster

upgrades.name: Heat Sink
upgrades.statChanges: +1 attack speed (6 total), +2 range (8 total), +150 HP

upgrades.name: Meltdown Core
upgrades.statChanges: +5 damage (16 total), max ramp bonus doubled, +2 armor

abilities.name: Heat Ramp
abilities.description: Damage increases by 10% for each consecutive second the beam stays on the same target, up to +100% (doubling base damage). Resets when switching targets. Additionally, the beam melts armor: after 3 consecutive seconds on the same target, reduces the target's armor by 1 every 2 seconds (stacks indefinitely, persists for 5 seconds after beam leaves). This allows the Inferno Tower to burn through heavily armored targets given enough time, and benefits other towers attacking the same target.

resource.name: Leach Tower
resource.description: Shoots a ray at enemy bodies (persist ~10sec after death). Converts bodies into Materials at 1 material per 10 HP the enemy had. Conversion takes 0.3 sec per body. Essential for late-game material income. Place near kill zones for maximum efficiency.
resource.visualDescription: Tall narrow lattice tower with an open skeletal frame of dark metal struts, topped by a wide concave collection dish that rotates slowly. The dish emits a focused green extraction beam downward onto enemy corpses. Harvested material particles (glowing green motes) visibly travel up the beam and collect in the dish, which pulses brighter with each successful harvest. Corpses being harvested visibly dissolve -- flesh and bone breaking down into the green particle stream over 0.3 seconds. Dark iron frame with green energy strips running up the lattice struts. Idle animation (no corpses): dish rotates scanning the ground, green highlights dim to a faint pulse. Collection sound: a rising crystalline chime on each completed harvest.
resource.effectDescription: Targets enemy corpses within range. Converts 1 material per 10 HP the enemy originally had. Conversion takes 0.3 seconds per body. Bodies persist for ~10 seconds after death. One Leach Tower can only process one body at a time.

upgrades.name: Rapid Extraction
upgrades.statChanges: Conversion time reduced to 0.15 sec per body. +4 range (14 total).

upgrades.name: Deep Harvest
upgrades.statChanges: Extraction rate doubled: 1 material per 5 HP. Can process 2 bodies simultaneously.

resource.name: Thermal Siphon
resource.description: Continuously drains Energy from living enemies in range. Deals low damage as a side effect. Unlike the Leach Tower, this works on living enemies -- place it where enemies spend the most time (near barriers they're attacking). Generates energy proportional to enemy density in range.
resource.visualDescription: Tapered conical tower wider at the base, narrowing to a spherical energy collector orb at the top. Smooth white ceramic-like exterior panels with inset cyan energy channels running vertically down the surface in 4 evenly-spaced lines. The top orb pulses with cyan light, intensity increasing with more enemies in range. Visible wisps of blue-white energy are pulled from nearby enemies (appearing as thin luminous tendrils stretching from each enemy to the orb). Affected enemies show a brief cyan shimmer on their body. Idle animation (no enemies): orb dims to a slow soft pulse, energy channels flow with minimal glow. Active animation: orb brightens significantly, channels surge with flowing energy, multiple tendrils connect to enemies simultaneously. Distinctly different silhouette from the Repair Tower (conical vs squat drum shape).
resource.effectDescription: Drains 0.5 energy per second from each enemy within range (no limit on simultaneous targets). Deals 5 damage per second to the primary target as a side effect. 10 enemies in range = 5 energy/sec passive income.

upgrades.name: Improved Collectors
upgrades.statChanges: Drain rate increased to 0.8 energy/sec per enemy. +3 range (15 total).

upgrades.name: Overcharged Siphon
upgrades.statChanges: Drain rate 1.2 energy/sec per enemy. Side-effect damage increased to 12/sec. Drains from all enemies simultaneously.

support.name: Repair Tower
support.description: Emits a repair field that heals all friendly buildings and units within its radius. Stacks with Repair Drone healing and passive regen. Essential for sustaining frontline barriers and towers under heavy fire. Place behind your defensive line for maximum coverage.
support.visualDescription: Squat, wide drum-shaped tower (shorter and wider than other 1x1 towers to distinguish its silhouette). Smooth white exterior with a broad rotating ring of 6 green emitter nodes circling the flat top. The emitter ring spins continuously, releasing visible green healing pulse waves that expand outward in concentric rings. Buildings and units being healed show green cross-shaped particle effects briefly on contact with each pulse. Clean medical/support aesthetic -- white panels with green accent markings and a central green status light. Idle animation: ring spins slowly, pulses are gentle and widely spaced. Under heavy use: ring spins faster, pulses are more frequent and brighter. Distinctly different from the Thermal Siphon (drum shape vs conical shape, green vs cyan).
support.effectDescription: Heals all friendly buildings and units within 6 grid units for 8 HP/sec. Stacks with passive regen and Repair Drone healing. Does not stack with other Repair Towers (highest value wins).

upgrades.name: Expanded Field
upgrades.statChanges: +3 effect radius (9 total), +3 HP/sec (11 total)

upgrades.name: Nanite Swarm
upgrades.statChanges: +5 HP/sec (16 total), now stacks with other Repair Towers, +100 HP

support.name: War Beacon
support.description: Projects a targeting enhancement field that increases the damage output of all towers within its radius. The core force multiplier -- a single War Beacon boosting 4-5 offensive towers is more efficient than building another offensive tower. Place centrally among tower clusters.
support.visualDescription: Tall open-lattice antenna tower made of dark triangular metal trusses (radio tower style silhouette), topped by a bright pulsing red beacon orb. Four vertical red LED light strips run up the corners of the lattice frame. The beacon emits a visible circular red energy field at ground level showing the buff radius. Towers within the buff radius gain a subtle red glow on their barrels/emitters and a small red upward-arrow icon above them. Dark oxidized metal construction with industrial bolted joints. Idle animation: beacon pulses steadily every 2 seconds, light strips glow in sync. Active (towers in radius): field is visible as a translucent red disc at ground level, beacon pulses brighter.
support.effectDescription: +15% damage to all friendly towers within 8 grid units. Does not stack with other War Beacons. Also applies to player units within radius.

upgrades.name: Amplified Signal
upgrades.statChanges: +10% damage buff (25% total), +2 radius (10 total)

upgrades.name: War Cry Protocol
upgrades.statChanges: +10% damage buff (35% total), now also grants +10% attack speed to towers in radius

support.name: Targeting Array
support.description: Advanced sensor suite that extends the effective range of all nearby towers. Lets towers engage enemies earlier and cover more ground. Particularly powerful with Rail Guns and Plasma Mortars that benefit most from extra range. Place near your longest-range towers.
support.visualDescription: Tall single-pole mast with a large parabolic radar dish mounted on top that rotates continuously. The dish is a smooth white composite with a cyan receiver array in the center. Below the dish, a ring of small holographic projectors generates floating cyan targeting reticle symbols and range-arc indicators around the tower (simple geometric shapes -- circles, crosshairs, distance markers). Cyan sensor beams sweep outward from the dish as it rotates, visible as thin laser-like lines. Towers within buff radius show a cyan range circle on the ground around them (player feedback for extended range). Sleek white pole with flush-mounted cyan data panels. Idle animation: dish rotates at steady pace, holographic elements drift slowly. Active: dish rotation speeds up slightly, holographic reticle symbols pulse with each sweep.
support.effectDescription: +20% range to all friendly towers within 8 grid units. Does not stack with other Targeting Arrays. Visually shows extended range circles on affected towers.

upgrades.name: Enhanced Sensors
upgrades.statChanges: +10% range buff (30% total), +2 effect radius (10 total)

upgrades.name: Predictive Tracking
upgrades.statChanges: +10% range buff (40% total), towers in radius also gain +15% projectile speed (less dodging)

support.name: Shield Pylon
support.description: Generates a protective energy field that hardens all nearby buildings, granting bonus armor. Critical for keeping frontline barriers and towers alive during heavy assaults. The armor bonus is especially effective against fast-attacking swarm enemies where each point of armor blocks more total damage.
support.visualDescription: hexagonal pylon with a shimmering blue-white energy dome projected upward, visible shield hexagons appear over nearby buildings, glowing blue crystal embedded in the top, reinforced plated base
support.effectDescription: +3 armor to all friendly buildings within 6 grid units. Stacks with other Shield Pylons (max +6 from pylons). Does not affect player units, only buildings.

upgrades.name: Extended Field
upgrades.statChanges: +3 effect radius (9 total), +1 armor buff (+4 total)

upgrades.name: Hardlight Barrier
upgrades.statChanges: +2 armor buff (+6 total), now also affects player units in radius, +150 HP

special.name: Central Tower
special.description: The heart of the base. If destroyed, the game is lost. Can be upgraded 3 tiers (requires boss kills: 1 kill for Tier 2, 10 kills for Tier 3). Each tier doubles resource income rate. Upgrading also unlocks new towers and units gated behind tech tiers. Protect it at all costs.
special.visualDescription: Massive three-tiered stepped pyramid command center occupying a 3x3 grid. Bottom tier is a reinforced bunker base with thick segmented blast doors and armored panel walls showing heavy riveting. Middle tier is a command deck with narrow slit windows glowing from interior holographic displays. Top tier is a sensor platform with 4 rotating satellite dishes at the corners and a central communications spire. Exterior is dark gunmetal with panel lines and maintenance markings. Tier upgrade visuals: Tier 1 adds blue energy conduit lines running up the walls and a blue holographic shield shimmer over the structure. Tier 2 replaces blue with purple, adds floating holographic tactical displays orbiting the top tier, and extends the spire with additional antenna. Tier 3 shifts to gold, the entire structure gains a visible energy aura, all holographic elements intensify, and the spire emits a steady upward beam of golden light. Damage states: at 75% HP exterior panels crack and sparks fly, at 50% HP sections of wall are visibly breached with interior fires visible, at 25% HP the structure is heavily damaged with flickering power and intermittent holographic failures. Ambient effects: steady low hum, blinking status lights, slow dish rotation, and faint holographic data scrolling on exterior panels.
special.effectDescription: Starts built. Each upgrade tier doubles resource income rate (Tier 1: 2x, Tier 2: 4x, Tier 3: 8x base rate). Unlocks tech-gated towers and units.

upgrades.name: Tier 1 Tech
upgrades.statChanges: Doubles income (2x base). Unlocks Tier 1 towers/units. Requires: 1 Boss Kill. +500 HP (2000 total), +2 armor.

upgrades.name: Tier 2 Tech
upgrades.statChanges: Doubles income again (4x base). Unlocks Tier 2 towers/units. Requires: 10 Boss Kills. +500 HP (2500 total), +3 armor.

upgrades.name: Tier 3 Tech
upgrades.statChanges: Doubles income again (8x base). Unlocks Tier 3 upgrades and meta-progression content. Requires: 25 Boss Kills. +500 HP (3000 total), +5 armor. Grants all production building upgrades at max level for free.
